title: VPN Client Version Compliance
spl:
index=vpn (sourcetype="cisco:asa" OR sourcetype="pan:globalprotect") action="session_connect" earliest=-24h
| eval major_minor=replace(client_version,"^(\d+\.\d+).*","\1")
| lookup vpn_min_client.csv platform OUTPUT min_version
| eval compliant=if(major_minor>=min_version,1,0)
| where compliant=0
| stats count by user, client_version, platform
| sort -count
description: Reports AnyConnect/GlobalProtect client versions against minimum supported builds.
value: IT security teams track VPN client version compliance across the endpoint fleet, ensuring end-of-life clients with known vulnerabilities are upgraded before they become attack vectors.
implementation: Use `ver` normalisation or `version` field if numeric. Block or warn via posture integration.
visualization: Pie chart (compliant vs not), Table (outdated clients), Bar chart (by version).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (`sourcetype=cisco:asa`) or Palo Alto Networks Add-on (`sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`). Data in `index=vpn`.
• Key fields: `user`, `client_version` (AnyConnect version, GlobalProtect version), `os_type`, `action=session_connect`.
• On Cisco ASA, AnyConnect version appears in session connect events (message ID 722022/722032).
• Create a `vpn_target_version.csv` lookup: `os_type,target_version,min_version,eol_version`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify client version data:
```spl
index=vpn (sourcetype="cisco:asa" OR sourcetype="pan:globalprotect") action="session_connect" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(user) as users by client_version
| sort client_version
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Version compliance:
```spl
index=vpn (sourcetype="cisco:asa" OR sourcetype="pan:globalprotect") action="session_connect" earliest=-7d
| eval major_minor=replace(client_version, "^(\d+\.\d+).*", "\1")
| stats dc(user) as users latest(_time) as last_seen by client_version, major_minor
| lookup vpn_target_version.csv OUTPUT target_version min_version eol_version
| eval compliance=case(client_version=target_version, "CURRENT", client_version<min_version, "OUTDATED", client_version=eol_version, "EOL", 1==1, "ACCEPTABLE")
| stats sum(users) as total_users by compliance
```

Understanding this SPL: Categorizes VPN client versions against the target, minimum, and end-of-life thresholds. EOL clients may have known vulnerabilities. The compliance rate shows upgrade campaign effectiveness. Splitting by `major_minor` helps identify which release branch is behind.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Check your own AnyConnect version (Help > About) and compare with Splunk.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Pie chart of compliance breakdown. Table of EOL/outdated users.
Alert: Weekly compliance report. Immediate for EOL clients connecting.

Runbook:
1. **EOL clients** — Push auto-update via AnyConnect headend update or SCCM deployment.
2. **Compliance rate declining** — Auto-update may be failing. Check the ASA AnyConnect image configuration.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **`client_version` null** — Not all ASA log messages include the version. Look specifically at message ID 722032.
• **Version format varies** — Different platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux, mobile) may use different version strings.
grandmaExplanation: We check that everyone's VPN software on their laptop is up to date, because old versions might have security holes that attackers could exploit.
